body:
# Feature request

## Summary

Make sure all navigation menu content is rendered to the HTML sent to the browser.

## Examples in other libraries

Radix UI made this possible by using `forceMount=true` on the `Content` component: https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/navigation-menu

## Motivation

For SEO purposes, I would like my entire navigation structure be present in raw HTML of my website. The way NavigationMenu is implemented now, this does not seem possible (the "drop down areas" / Content, are not visible). This means unfortunately for now I can't migrate from Radix to Base UI

comments.body:
You can use `keepMounted` on `<NavigationMenu.Portal>`

Google and other web crawlers wait for hydration, so they'll see the content despite it not being in the initial HTML (due to the portal):

```jsx
<NavigationMenu.Portal keepMounted>
```

Thanks for the quick reply!

`keepMounted` only keeps the portal container. The actual items in the dropdown are only added to the DOM when I hover.

So it seems like `forceMount` in Radix fixes two things:

- the dropdown content is always available in the DOM, regardless of hover state (critical)
- the dropdown content is sent as HTML over the wire, so web crawlers don't need to wait for hydration (I agree this might not be a big deal at this day and age) (nice to have)

Curious if there's a solution that addresses both!

Yeah sorry, you're right

A relatively simple solution would likely be to render the children present in `<NavigationMenu.Content>` in a `<div hidden>` parent container which should solve both of those concerns. (You can also do this yourself externally as a workaround currently)
Thanks. Do you think support will be added in base-ui? if so, I'd rather hold off on any workarounds for now
Yeah, we'll fix this before the next release
@atomiks This would be super useful, is this still planned for the next release?

This fix will be available in the next npm release of Base UI.

In the meantime, you can try it out on our Canary release channel:

```sh
npm i https://pkg.pr.new/@base-ui/react@3794
```

labels.name: type: enhancement
labels.description: It’s an improvement, but we can’t make up our mind whether it's a bug fix or a new feature.

labels.name: has workaround
labels.description: There’s a bug, but users have a complete workaround, so no urgent fix or release is needed.

labels.name: component: navigation menu
labels.description: Changes related to the navigation menu component.

title: [navigation menu] support for SSR / forceMount
